'''Trazzen''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and commanding [[Officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Obliterator]]'' of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Starfleet]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]] some [[19 ABY/Legends|fifteen years after]] the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Trazzen had a reputation for being soft-spoken belying his reputation for ruthlessness. He was one of four [[Braxant Sector Fleet]] officers whose Star Destroyers were chosen to serve [[Flim (Human)|Flim]], a con-artist portraying a returned [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], and the plans of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Vilim Disra|Disra]] and [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guardsman]] [[Grodin Tierce]] to envelope the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] into a civil war over the crisis concerning the revealed [[Caamas Document]]. In order to convince Trazzen and his fellow officers—captains [[Nalgol]] of the ''[[Tyrannic]]'', [[Argona]] of the [[Ironhand (Imperial-class)|''Ironhand'']], and [[Dorja]] of the [[Relentless (Dorja)|''Relentless'']]—that Flim was Thrawn, Disra had a meeting executed where the four captains personally met with the Thrawn-apparent. Of the four Star Destroyers, the ''Tyrannic'', ''Obliterator'', and ''Ironhand'' were chosen to lead a assault on the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] homeworld of [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]], in which the assault was foiled and the Star Destroyers were forced to withdraw. After that event, Trazzen's fate is unknown. 

==Personality and traits==
Trazzen was soft-spoken, but had a reputation for being vicious.

*''[[Specter of the Past]]'' {{Ab|Specter of the Past (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}}
